title: Adjustable trial neck
abstract: A kit of parts and method for Mailing a hip joint are described. The kit of parts comprises a first femoral part (170) having a first attachment formation (175) at a superior end, a second femoral part (180) having a second female formation (185) at a superior end and a trial neck (100). The trial neck has a neck attachment formation (106) at a first end (108) arranged to releasably attach the trial neck to the first attachment formation at a first position or the second attachment formation at a second position. The first position and the second position correspond to different neck lengths. The trial neck also has a head attachment formation (102) at a free end (104) for releasably attaching a trial femoral head (162) and an adjustment mechanism (130) operable by a user to vary the position of the head attachment formation relative to the neck attachment formation in a direction transverse to a femoral longitudinal axis (172) of the first femoral part or the second femoral part to adjust the neck offset provided by the trial neck when attached in use to the first femoral part or the second femoral part.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A kit of parts for Mailing a hip joint, comprising: a first femoral part having a first attachment formation at a superior end; a second femoral part having a second female formation at a superior end; and a trial neck having a neck attachment formation at a first end arranged to releasably attach the trial neck to the first attachment formation at a first position or the second attachment formation at a second position, wherein the first position and the second position correspond to different neck lengths, a head attachment formation at a free end for releasably attaching a trial femoral head, and an adjustment mechanism operable by a user to vary the position of the head attachment formation relative to the neck attachment formation in a direction transverse to a femoral longitudinal axis of the first femoral part or the second femoral part to adjust the neck offset provided by the trial neck when attached in use to the first femoral part or the second femoral part. 2. The kit of parts of claim 1, wherein the first attachment formation is a first female formation, the second attachment formation is a second female formation and the neck attachment formation is a male formation. 3. The kit of parts of claim 2, wherein the first female formation has a first depth, the second female formation has a second depth and the first depth and the second depth are different depths. 4. The kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the first femoral part and the second femoral part have different sizes. 5. The kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the first femoral part is a first femoral broach and the second femoral part is a second femoral broach. 6. The kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the trial neck has a neck axis extending longitudinally to the trial neck and wherein when the trial neck is attached in use to the first femoral part or the second femoral part, the femoral longitudinal axis and neck axis subtend an obtuse angle in the range of 125Â° to 135Â°. 7. The kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the trial neck has a neck axis extending longitudinally to the trial neck and wherein the adjustment mechanism is configured to translate the head attachment formation along an adjustment axis, and 5 wherein the neck axis and adjustment axis subtend and acute angle in the range of 35Â° to 45Â°. 8. The kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 7, wherein the adjustment mechanism is configured to be operable by a user to vary the neck offset continuously. 10 9. The kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 8, wherein the adjustment mechanism includes an external interface arranged to receive a tool to operate the adjustment mechanism. 15 10. The kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 9, wherein the adjustment mechanism comprises a rack and pinion. 1 1. The kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 10, and further including a tool for operating the adjustment mechanism. 20 12. The kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 1 1, and further including a trial femoral head having a bore for receiving the head attachment formation to releasably attach the trial femoral head to the trial neck. 25 13. The kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 12, and further including a prosthetic femoral implant including a femoral neck having a neck geometry, and wherein the neck geometry is reproducible by an assembly of the trial neck and the first femoral part or the second femoral part. 30 14. The kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 12, and further including a plurality of prosthetic femoral implants each including a femoral neck and each having a respective neck geometry, and wherein each respective neck geometry is reproducible by an assembly of the trial neck and the first femoral part or the second femoral part. 15. An assembly of the kit of parts of any of claims 1 to 14, wherein the trial neck is attached to a selected one of the first femoral part and the second femoral part. 5 16. A method of trialling a hip joint during a hip replacement procedure being carried out on a patient, comprising: attaching a trial neck to a selected femoral part of a plurality of femoral parts, each corresponding to a different sized femoral implant; reducing a trial hip joint including a trial femoral head mounted on the trial neck; 10 adjusting the trial neck to change the neck offset; and assessing the trial hip joint. 17. The method of claim 16, and further comprising: shortening the length of the trial neck before reducing the trial hip joint. 15 18. The method of claim 16 or 17, and further comprising: shortening the length of the trial neck after reducing the trial hip joint; and dislocating the trial hip joint. 20 19. The method of any of claims 16 to 18, and further comprising: assessing the tension of the trial hip joint after reducing the trial hip joint. 20. The method of any of claims 16 to 19, and further comprising: further adjusting the trial neck to change the neck offset; and 25 further assessing the trial hip joint 21. The method of any of claims 16 to 20, wherein a tool is used to adjust the neck offset or shorten the length of the trial neck while the trial hip joint is reduced. 30 22. The method of any of claims 16 to 21 , further comprising: establishing a target geometry of the trial hip joint after adjusting the trial neck to change the neck offset; 

 providing a femoral implant having a femoral neck arranged to reproduce the target geometry; and implanting the femoral implant after the trial neck has been removed. 23. The method of any of claims 16 to 22, wherein the femoral part is a femoral broach. 24. The method of claim 23, and further comprising: removing a first broach from a femur of the patient; using a second broach having a different size to the first broach to prepare the femur; and reattaching the trial neck to the second broach while the second broach is within the femur.
